rationale: Validator check passes with exit 0 and zero errors; the only issue is the known WARN decomposition-scope-redundancy R=0.08. I parsed the plan frontmatter directly: work_chunks ids are contiguous 1..4 and each declares depends_on: []. Spot-runs from the worktree root: go build ./... exits 0 (with a sandbox cache-write warning only), go test ./internal/setup/... -run RefusesSymlinked passes, and go test ./internal/complete/... ./internal/phase/... passes. The exact deadcode go run command is named correctly but cannot complete in this sandbox because proxy.golang.org DNS/network access is unavailable and no local deadcode binary/cache is present; I treat this as an environment limitation, not a plan defect. The plan remains aligned to the approved spec and its verification commands are otherwise executable as written.

findings.observed: mindspec validate plan 107-cleanup-deadcode-dry-wave1 exits 0. JSON/text output report one WARN only: decomposition-scope-redundancy R=0.08 below threshold 0.15.
Parsed work_chunks directly from the YAML frontmatter: ids are 1, 2, 3, 4 and every chunk has depends_on: [].
findings.impact: Satisfies the requested contiguous independent bead cut.

findings.observed: go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@latest -test ./cmd/... ./internal/... ./plugins/... failed before analysis with DNS failure resolving proxy.golang.org; which deadcode found no local binary.
findings.impact: The command is named in runnable Go syntax, but this restricted sandbox cannot fetch @latest. Other exact verification commands ran successfully.
